Cártel de Tláhuac.description: Mexican criminal group founded in 2012 by Felipe de Jesús Pérez Luna, alias El Ojos, operating primarily in Tláhuac, Iztapalapa, Milpa Alta, Xochimilco, and the State of Mexico. It emerged from splinter groups of the Beltrán Leyva Cartel and engages in drug trafficking, homicide, extortion, kidnapping, vehicle theft, fuel theft, and human trafficking. The group is currently led by relatives of El Ojos, including his wife and children.

descriptions: Cártel de Tláhuac was created from splinters of Cártel de los Beltrán Leyva.
edges.evidence_quote: Este grupo fue creado a partir de varias escisiones del Cártel de los Beltrán Leyva

Giuliano clan.description: The Clan Giuliano is a Camorra organization based in central Naples, founded by Pio Vittorio Giuliano in the 1950s. It was one of the most powerful criminal groups in Campania from the 1970s to the 1990s, involved in cigarette smuggling, drug trafficking, and counterfeiting. The clan operated in neighborhoods such as Forcella, San Gaetano, and Piazza Mercato. Several family members became pentiti (collaborators with justice) in the early 2000s, leading to a decline in its power.

descriptions: Paranza dei Bambini is the group formed by Giuliano, Sibillo, and others.
edges.evidence_quote: il gruppo Giuliano-Sibillo-Brunetti-Amirante, meglio noto come Paranza dei Bambini

descriptions: Giuliano clan is a constituent clan of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: the Giuliano clan, from Forcella, named after Pio Vittorio Giuliano;

descriptions: Cappello clan has ties with Giuliano clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan Cappello vanta legami anche con altri gruppi criminali ... clan Giuliano di Pachino (SR)

descriptions: The Di Biasi acted as the longa manus (extended arm) of the Giuliano clan in the Quartieri Spagnoli.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni '80 i Di Biasi erano la longa manus dei Giuliano nei Quartieri Spagnoli.

descriptions: Initially allied with Luigi Giuliano's clan, but later the relationship dissolved into a feud.
edges.evidence_quote: amico fin dall'inizio della sua carriera criminale di Luigi Giuliano, legame che poi si sarebbe sciolto e avrebbe determinato una faida.

edges.evidence_quote: The third generation of the Giulianos along with other small groups aligned to them, such as the Amirante-Brunetti-Sibillo, is dubbed by the media 'Paranza dei bambini'.

descriptions: Both were members of the Nuova Famiglia alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: the two organizations have always been rivals, which culminated in several episodes of violence in the 2000s.

descriptions: Initially cooperative, then turned to conflict after Cutolo demanded a cut from Giuliano's gambling.
edges.evidence_quote: The Giuliano clan had been in good terms with the Nuova Camorra Organizzata, headed by Raffaele Cutolo until the first half of 1979, but the two organizations then broke out into conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In questo contesto i fratelli Giuliano si inseriscono stabilmente nell'organizzazione detta Nuova Famiglia , creata con il solo intento di distruggere Cutolo.

edges.evidence_quote: Gennaro Licciardi ... was the capozona in the district of Secondigliano for the Giuliano clan of Forcella. At that time, Licciardi was the second-in-command to boss Luigi Giuliano.

descriptions: Gennaro Licciardi was a capozona for Clan Giuliano before forming his own clan.

descriptions: Mazzarella clan strengthened relations with Giuliano clan through marriage.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1996 Marianna, daughter of Luigi Giuliano , boss of the Giuliano clan , married Michele Mazzarella, son of Vincenzo Mazzarella, the union served to strengthen the relations between the two most important clans of Camorra in the 1990s.

descriptions: Mazzarella was allied with Giuliano for a short time.

edges.evidence_quote: The Georgian mafia has two major criminal clans from Tbilisi and Kutaisi.

descriptions: Kutaisi and Tbilisi clans are in a violent feud.
edges.evidence_quote: In more recent years, the Georgian underworld in Russia, Georgia and elsewhere has been characterized by a violent feud between the Kutaisi and Tbilisi clans, led by Tariel Oniani and Aslan Usoyan, respectively.

Ramacca Mafia family.description: The Ramacca Mafia family is a crime family of Cosa Nostra based in Ramacca, Sicily, founded in the 1950s by Calogero 'Liddu' Conti. It engages in extortion, infiltration of public contracts, and political influence. The family has maintained control over local economic interests and allied with powerful neighboring families. It remains active, with recent reorganizations under Pasquale Oliva and Rosario Di Dio.

descriptions: Ramacca Mafia family is listed as an ally in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: The MB dominated the Islamic Society of Britain (ISB)

Rebels Motorcycle Club.description: The Rebels Motorcycle Club is an Australian outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1969 by Clint Jacks in Brisbane, Queensland. At its peak, it was the largest motorcycle club in Australia with over 1,000 members. The club is considered a criminal organization by Australian authorities, involved in drug trafficking, arms dealing, and other crimes, though it claims to be a group of motorcycle enthusiasts.

descriptions: Bandidos consider Rebels as rivals in Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the Comancheros , Finks , Gypsy Jokers , Hells Angels , Mongols , Notorious , Rebels and Red Devils

descriptions: Rebels MC is listed as a rival of Bandidos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Cobra, Comanchero MC, Cossacks MC, Finks MC, Gremium MC, Gypsy Joker MC, Hells Angels MC, Kinfolk MC, Mongols MC, No Surrender MC, Notorious MC, Rebels MC, Red Devils MC, Rock Machine MC, Satudarah MC, Vagos MC

descriptions: Rebels MC attacked Bandidos clubhouse shortly after its founding.
edges.evidence_quote: Shortly after the founding of the Helsingborg Bandidos, the Rebels – a Hells Angels support club at that point – carried out a shooting attack on the Bandidos' clubhouse.

descriptions: The text lists the Rebels as rivals of the Bandidos.
edges.evidence_quote: The Australian Bandidos are allied with the Diablos , Mobshitters and Rock Machine , while their rivals include the Comancheros , Finks , Gypsy Jokers , Hells Angels , Mongols , Notorious , Rebels

descriptions: Rebels members killed three Bandidos members in 1997, indicating a violent conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Bandidos members Michael "Kaos" Kulakowski, Saša "Sash" Milenković and Rick Raymond de Stoop were shot dead in the basement of a nightclub in Chippendale , Sydney in the early hours of 10 November 1997 by Konstantinos "Kon" Georgiou and Bruce Malcolm Harrison. Georgiou was a serving member of the Rebels at the time, while Harrison was a former member.

descriptions: Drive-by shootings in 2008 were linked to a dispute between Bandidos and Rebels.
edges.evidence_quote: Four Bandidos members – Joshua Clark, Bradley John Duff, Malcolm Sinclair Greig and Todd Obierzynski – were arrested after being found in possession of two semi-automatic rifles when they were stopped by police in a routine traffic check in Sydney's west while travelling home after carrying out two drive-by shootings on 10 December 2008. Police believed the shootings, carried out in Lurnea and Sadleir , to be linked to a dispute between the Bandidos and Rebels.

descriptions: Bandidos firebombed a Rebels clubhouse in 2007 in retaliation for an assault.
edges.evidence_quote: Bandidos Brisbane chapter president Blair Raymond Thomsen, vice-president Ivan Glavas, sergeant-at-arms Kenneth James Whittaker and John Debilla, an associate and disgruntled former Rebels member, were convicted in June 2008 of firebombing a Rebels clubhouse in Albion , Brisbane on 27 March 2007. The Bandidos had carried out the attack as retribution after Rebels members assaulted Bandidos bikies with baseball bats at Bribie Island in February 2007, leaving one man critically injured.

descriptions: Rebels members shot a Bandidos member in 2019 during a pre-arranged meeting.
edges.evidence_quote: Seven Rebels members were charged after Harley Cranston, a member of the Bandidos' Beenleigh chapter who was allegedly brandishing a machete, was shot and wounded in the leg at the Logan Hyperdome in the Logan City suburb of Shailer Park on 4 February 2019.

descriptions: Bandidos consider Rebels as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the ... Rebels

descriptions: A Bandidos chapter patched over to Rebels in 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: The Perth Bandidos chapter patched over to the Rebels in June 2017 after senior Rebels members came to an amicable agreement with the Bandidos' eastern states -based leadership.

descriptions: Brother Speed allegedly sent methamphetamine to the Rebels in Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Brothers Speed in Idaho have allegedly been involved in sending methamphetamine, sealed inside the frames of motorcycles, to the Rebels in Sydney, Australia.

descriptions: The infobox lists Rebels as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kings Crew, Rebels, Outcasts, Warlords, RCMP

descriptions: The Grim Reapers were one of four dominant clubs in Alberta, alongside Rebels, Warlords, and King's Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Along with the Rebels, the Warlords, and King's Crew, they were one of the four dominant outlaw motorcycle clubs operating in Alberta prior to 1997.

descriptions: Hells Angels in conflict with Rebels over colors.
edges.evidence_quote: The first conflict was with the Rebels after the Hells Angels demanded that the Rebels change their colors from red and white (the same color as the Hells Angels).

descriptions: Rebels joined Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: in 1998, the Rebels of Saskatoon, Saskatchewan joined.

descriptions: Kings Crew and Rebels were allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Rebels Motorcycle Club

descriptions: A Loners chapter in Saskatoon patched over to the Rebels MC.
edges.evidence_quote: However, it would 'Patch-Over' to the Alberta-based Rebels MC.

descriptions: Notorious competes with traditional bikie gangs like Rebels for drug turf.
edges.evidence_quote: Notorious, a recent ethnically Middle Eastern based gang, have started competing with existing traditionally Anglo-Celtic bikie gangs, in a turf war for drug sales.

descriptions: Rebels associates killed a Bandidos member, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Two Rebel associates were arrested for the murder of Bandidos member Ross Brand after their clubhouse was raided, on 16 November 2008.

descriptions: The Rebels Edmonton chapter attempted to patch over to the Bandidos in 2001.
edges.evidence_quote: Joey 'Crazy Horse' Morin, Vice President acting on behalf of Pete Sorenson (president) for the Edmonton chapter of the Rebels Motorcycle Club, first contacted the Bandidos with the aim of 'patching over'

descriptions: The Rebels and Grim Reapers were rivals with violent clashes in the 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1970s the Rebels had multiple violent exchanges with their main rivals in the province- the Grim Reapers Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: A Gypsy Joker member and a Rebels member were jointly involved in a homicide, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: On 14 April 2012, Anthony Perish (a Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club member), his brother Andrew (a Rebels Motorcycle Club member) and Matthew Lawton were sentenced to eighteen, nine and fifteen years respectively imprisonment for the homicide of convicted Sydney drug trafficker Terry Falconer, as well as firearms and drug dealing offences.

descriptions: The Saskatoon chapter of the Rebels was patched over to the Hells Angels in 1998.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 1998, the Saskatoon Rebels were patched over to the Hells Angels

descriptions: Kings Crew is listed as an ally of the Rebels in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Satan's Choice , Kings Crew Motorcycle Club , Lobos and, later, the Hells Angels

descriptions: Lobos is listed as an ally of the Rebels in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Satan's Choice , Kings Crew Motorcycle Club , Lobos and, later, the Hells Angels

descriptions: The Rebels merged with the Loners Motorcycle Club in the 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: after merging with the Loners Motorcycle Club of Saskatchewan in the 1980s

descriptions: Violent feud between Rebels and Rock Machine involving firebombings, assaults, and assassination attempts.
edges.evidence_quote: The defection of Rebels MC members to the Rock Machine MC sparked an ongoing violent feud between the groups, when the Rock Machine settled in Perth in 2009 there were allegations by media that a turf war broke out between the two rival motorcycle clubs, with exchanges between the two groups including firebombings, assaults and the assassination attempt in 2011 of Rebels WA president Nick Martin, who survived being shot, tensions remain ongoing.

descriptions: Satan's Choice is listed as an ally of the Rebels in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Satan's Choice , Kings Crew Motorcycle Club , Lobos and, later, the Hells Angels

descriptions: Warlocks Motorcycle Club (Florida) is listed as a rival of the Rebels in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Grim Reapers , Warlocks Motorcycle Club (Florida) , EPS , RCMP , Canadian Airborne Regiment

descriptions: Rock Machine and Rebels MC became rivals after Rock Machine expanded into Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: The rivalry between the Rock Machine and the Rebels Motorcycle Club started when the Canadian club expanded into Australia.

descriptions: Rebels attempted to recruit Tribesmen members through a patch-over.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2011, the newly established Rebels Motorcycle Club New Zealand Chapter voiced its intentions to 'patch over' the Tribesmen Motorcycle Club.

Gopher Gang.description: Early 20th-century New York street gang based in Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan. Formed in the 1890s from local gangs, numbering around 500 members. Controlled territory from Fourth to Forty-Second Street and Seventh to Eleventh Avenue. Engaged in burglary, armed robbery, assault, prostitution, and illegal gambling. Declined in the 1910s.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang was strongly rivaled by the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Five Points Gang (led by Paul Kelly) would rise to prominence during the early 1900s, strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters, the Gopher Gang, and others during the period.

descriptions: Allied gang listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gopher Gang

descriptions: Hudson Dusters and Gopher Gang were allies during gang wars against Potashes and Boodle Gangs.

descriptions: The text states Neighbors' Sons were rivals of the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Neighbors' Sons was a New York street gang which, operating in the neighborhoods between Bleecker and Grove Streets , were rivals of the Gopher Gang and the Hudson Dusters during the early 1900s.

Ryno-Skachevsky gang.description: Russian racist serial killer group composed of young men, headed by Artur Ryno and Pavel Skachevsky. Active in the 2000s, the gang targeted and murdered immigrants from Caucasus and Central Asia in Moscow. Members were convicted in 2008 for multiple murders and attacks. The group is now defunct.

36 Boys.description: The 36 Boys were a primarily Turkish immigrant gang based in Berlin-Kreuzberg, Germany, active from the late 1980s to the mid-1990s. Founded by Attila Murat Aydın, the gang engaged in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and other crimes. They marked their territory with graffiti and fought turf battles with Nazis, skinheads, and rival gangs. After dissolution, some former members turned to lawful careers or social work.

descriptions: The 36 Boys and Autonomists had a short-lived alliance during May riots.
edges.evidence_quote: During the May riots in Kreuzberg, the 36 Boys initially joined the Autonomists, but the alliance was short-lived because of the group's lack of political orientation.

descriptions: The Black Panthers are a rival gang to the 36 Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Other rival gangs were the Warriors from the Schlesisches Tor and the Black Panthers from Wedding.

descriptions: The 36 Boys fought turf battles against Nazis.
edges.evidence_quote: In the beginning of the 1990s the 36 Boys fought against turf battles with Nazis and Skinheads in the Jungfernheide park.

descriptions: The 36 Boys fought turf battles against Skinheads.
edges.evidence_quote: In the beginning of the 1990s the 36 Boys fought against turf battles with Nazis and Skinheads in the Jungfernheide park.

descriptions: The Warriors are a rival gang to the 36 Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Other rival gangs were the Warriors from the Schlesisches Tor and the Black Panthers from Wedding.

Kizuna-kai.description: The Kizuna-kai is a yakuza organization based in Hyogo, Japan, formed on April 30, 2017 by defectors from the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi. It was originally named Ninkyo-dantai-Yamaguchi-gumi, then Ninkyo Yamaguchi-gumi, before adopting its current name in 2020. The group has about 50 members and 70 quasi-members, and is led by Yoshinori Oda. It is designated as a bōryokudan under Japanese law.

descriptions: Kizuna-kai and Ikeda-gumi are in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: These Yamaguchi-gumi and the three organizations that split from them are fighting each other.

descriptions: Infobox lists Ikeda-gumi as an ally of Kizuna-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Ikeda-gumi

edges.evidence_quote: Ikeda and others attended the formation ceremony of the Keiji Union, which had split from the 6th generation Yamaguchi-gumi-affiliated Tankai-ikka, and it is believed that this group also joined the Ninkyo-dantai-Yamaguchi-gumi.

Tonton Macoute.description: Haitian paramilitary and secret police force created in 1959 by dictator François Duvalier. Known for brutality, state terrorism, and assassinations, it was renamed the Volontaires de la Sécurité Nationale in 1970. Formally disbanded in 1986, but members continued terrorizing the country. Responsible for 30,000-60,000 deaths.

edges.target: Front for the Advancement and Progress of Haïti
descriptions: FRAPH is described as modern Tonton Macoutes, indicating it is a successor or continuation.
edges.evidence_quote: The most feared paramilitary group during the 1990s was the Front for the Advancement and Progress of Haïti (FRAPH), which Toronto Star journalist Linda Diebel described as modern Tonton Macoutes

descriptions: PUN is the parent paramilitary organization of the Tonton Macoute.
edges.evidence_quote: Parent paramilitary organization: PUN

Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club.description: The Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club is a one-percenter motorcycle club founded in San Bernardino, California on April 1, 1956. It has expanded internationally, with significant presence in Australia, the United States, Germany, and Norway. Known for criminal activities including drug trafficking, homicide, and arson, the club is considered one of Australia's most violent bikie gangs. It gained notoriety for the 2001 car bomb murder of former detective Don Hancock in Western Australia.

descriptions: Bandidos consider Gypsy Jokers as rivals in Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the Comancheros , Finks , Gypsy Jokers , Hells Angels , Mongols , Notorious , Rebels and Red Devils
